qa_pairs.question: What happens if the `LMOD_SYSTEM_DEFAULT_MODULES` environment variable is not defined?
qa_pairs.answer: If `LMOD_SYSTEM_DEFAULT_MODULES` is left undefined, our environment will define it to load the `StdEnv` module, which will load by default a version of the Intel compiler and a version of OpenMPI.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the `MODULERCFILE` environment variable?
qa_pairs.answer: The `MODULERCFILE` environment variable is used by Lmod to define the default version of modules and aliases.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user customize the default versions of modules and aliases using `MODULERCFILE`?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can define their own `modulerc` file and add it to the `MODULERCFILE` environment variable.

qa_pairs.question: What is the precedence of a user-defined `modulerc` file when added to the `MODULERCFILE` environment variable?
qa_pairs.answer: A user-defined `modulerc` file added to the `MODULERCFILE` environment variable will take precedence over what is defined in our environment.
